prompt: A system has 32-bit addresses and 2 GB (231) main memory where each page is 1 MB (220). How many entries of a page table will contain?
reference_answer: Each page is 1 MB (220), so it needs 20 bits as an offset. Thus a 32-bit address can have at most 12-bit (32-20=12) as the page number. Therefore a page table con have at most 2^12 = 4096 entries.
